home.heroDescription: Author, Writer, Photographer, Videographer, Artistic Manager, Film Critic, Traveler…
home.heroCaption: You have to live as many experiences as possible. Getting a tattoo is definitely one of them.
home.biographyDescription: "Writer of intimacy." Like a web marketing slogan, this concise phrase sums up the impulse behind a “narrative I” writing — an exploration of the soul and the body. Behind forms that are both beautiful and rebellious, liberated from both academies and laboratories, my novels dig underground tunnels into feminine psychology. I seek a fragment of truth, nothing more. My characters are not monolithic blocks. And neither am I…
home.carouselCaption: During a literary debate with Christine Aventin and Pascal Leclercq.
home.carouselDescription: Escaping literature — from its schizophrenic prison (between my real “I” and my imaginary “I”) — has thus become a matter of survival. I initially found an outlet in documentary-style writing, exploring some of my favorite themes such as cinema, rock, and sports… Yet the horizon extends far beyond the stories written in keyboard ink…
home.lastSectionCaption: “Keep me safe, Keep me wild”, comme le
home.lastSectionDescription: In reality, I see myself as a schizophrenic lost in this asylum called “Planet Earth.” My madness is the pursuit of impossible immortality. I’m obsessed with living several lives, since one alone is not enough (honestly, I ask: how can anyone accept living just one life — and such a short one, at that?).
home.finalDescription: So there you have it — the writer of intimacy has become a long-distance explorer. Through travel, I am reborn at times as a photographer, at times as a videographer, at times as an artistic manager… Well hidden beneath the thick wool of a nomadic black sheep who invites you to frolic with him across the green meadows of this website…

biography.quote1: “By the time you learn to live, it's already too late,” <bold>Louis Aragon</bold>
biography.quote2: “That’s the great stupidity of men, we always tell ourselves we have time... And then everything dies. We find ourselves following coffins.” <bold>Philippe Claudel</bold>
biography.introDescription: <bold>Birth</bold> in Saint-Trond, nine months after a concerted action by my very dear parents. The date will remain secret. Let’s simply place it at the end of what historians call the baby boom.
biography.introDescription2: <bold>Childhood</bold> in Liège. It's the time of football with the kids from the neighborhood. Every Saturday night with my father, I attend Standard matches. On Sundays, my mother takes me to the Vica ice cream parlor where I lick vanilla and chocolate cones that drip on my fingers. During the week, I play football with the neighborhood kids. A blessed era. Happy people have no stories. Which doesn't prevent one from loving to listen to them, read them, tell them...
biography.caption1: A young starlet during a casting in Brussels. That's my mum. A leading man in his garden, that's my dad. My roots. Those who gave me life.
biography.adolescence: <bold>Adolescence.</bold> It's the time of first musical passions. It's also the time of love, the time of friends and adventure. With Myriel, my first love, I quench a thirst for travel and rock. England, Germany, Switzerland, France, Netherlands: like groupies, we follow Siouxsie and The Banshees on tour. During these road trips alongside Myriel, I embrace the initiation into the talent of muses.
biography.studies: <bold>Studies.</bold> At the time, high-potential students had to go through this box that prolongs schooling, there is hardly any alternative. I hesitate: Romance Philology or psychology. Romance Philology is justified by the ardent desire to write books, psychology by the fact that the stories crowding my head all touch, closely or remotely, on madness. Unable to decide, I opt for... journalism which hardly interests me, but whose staggering emptiness allows for an easy success in two months of study, without giving up travel or romantic encounters.
biography.salvage: I salvage this risk-free academic path through Hadelin Trinon's teaching at the Film Museum. From Eisenstein to David Lynch, I learn to dissect films. A diploma thus certifies me as a "Film Historian".
biography.cinema: <bold>On the cinema side.</bold> I direct two short films funded by the Atelier des Jeunes Cinéastes. Traces must remain on old VHS tapes. I wish no one to lay hands on them.
biography.litterature: <bold>On the literature side.</bold> At university, I met Sabine who, by curious coincidence, also aims to "enter literature". We live together, travel together, even write together: a four-handed novel that we have the merit of completing. We call it <italic>L'Epistolière...</italic> This is followed by a pathetic attempt to settle in Paris, according to a common disease among budding writers who naively dream of the prestige of French publishing. Phew, <italic>L’Épistolière</italic> will not be published and will rest forever in a drawer for the good of humanity.
biography.cinema2: <bold>On the cinema side.</bold> Sabine and I go to Romania to shoot a documentary, to film liberating theater. We pack the cameras into our red Lada. But the journey ends in a ditch, all four wheels in the air. Injuries, debts, and separation follow.
biography.experimentYouthTitle: Experiments of a less tender youth.
biography.youthCaption: The Red Lada still whole, in Austria, on its way to its fatal destination — heading to Bucharest.
biography.experimentYouthDesc1: The awareness of my desires asserted itself: to write, always and forever. A first version of <italic>La Langoureuse</italic> takes shape. But the return to Liège, and thus to my roots, is accompanied by nocturnal outings. Perdition, joys, and intoxication in smoky bars distract me.
biography.experimentYouthDesc2: Before writing, one must first live, notably with Carolina who comes from Porto and sweeps me into her native Portugal, before I take her, as a backpacker, to sleep on the beaches of Spain and France. Nights under the stars encourage me to explore further.
biography.experimentYouthDesc3: In Peru, Bolivia, Guatemala, I discover the absolute freedom that traveling alone provides. But in Mexico, a certain Elizabeth encourages me to settle in Mexico City. I sell chocolates on the zócalo. While the urge to write remains, the question looms: where, when, how?
biography.adultDescription1: The 30-year-old clock rings with yet another return to Liège. It might be time to "drift" less, to finally earn money, and if possible to create. A vague resumption of <italic>La Langoureuse</italic>, second version, is however thwarted by my entry into the newspaper Le Matin as a film critic.
biography.adultDescription2: Beyond the significant interviews with Olivier Assayas, Wong Kar-Wai, Maggie Cheung, Élodie Bouchez, I put my pen at the service of cinephilia. I spend my days in dark theaters and my nights in the newsroom. I enjoy myself in crescendo, notably thanks to the freedom of speech granted to me by the head of the “Culture” section, oh thank you Demetrio Scagliola!
biography.adultTitle3: Side: “And I tell you: ‘You must break the chains of work’”
biography.adultDescription3: I still stay greedy for parties and wine. During a joyful night, I meet Christina who will gradually become my dragonfly. Under this nickname hides an ideal muse. She calms my demons, she gives me the strength to refocus on my youthful dreams. And when Le Matin ceases its activities, I feel enough nervous energy in me to leave the glamorous glitz of film festivals and take refuge in the small office of a house I just bought.
biography.homeCaption: My house on rue Naniot, in Liège. The 'small office' is located on the 2nd floor at the back.
biography.homeDesc1: This is a haven of peace and concentration. Certainly, I border on schizophrenia again by plunging my moods into the character of Maleea Lori Hall, but when a muse takes control of operations, she holds her artist in balance, she relentlessly indicates the course to follow.
biography.litteratureDesc1: I put a final point to <italic>La Langoureuse</italic>. The third version, definitive, receives the Publishing Assistance Prize from the Royal Academy of French Language. This honor gives me a certain independence, me who dreams of tracing my path outside the system. Long-time friends wait for me there: Jean-Pierre Closson and his financial trust, Philippe Bachy and his literary confidence.
biography.litteratureDesc2: No need to send the manuscript to the mailboxes of Parisian publishers, no need to slip into the social dinners where careers are made, I tried ten years earlier, I hated it. In 2003, a publication at the modest Éditions du Somnambule Équivoque me convient perfectly.
biography.litteratureDesc3: Everything follows in a waking dream. The writing of <italic>Alissia Lone</italic> comes to me like a feminist cry with a concise, dry, and raw style. I have found my literary voice: classicism is over, replaced by a rough prose, arid as psychic torment. While the form asserts itself in contemporaneity, the substance still consists of realizing a portrait of a woman through the meanders of thoughts in “survival” mode.
biography.litteratureDesc4: But for <italic>Alissia Lone</italic>, unlike Maleea Lori Hall, the violence (which strikes the heroine) does not come from within, but from three males in heat. In short, it's a “live rape” that Somnambule Équivoque published in 2004. The theme will naturally cause controversy: does a man have the right to be the spokesperson for the opposite sex, for a female brain, for a vagina?
biography.cinemaCaption: Place Saint Léonard in Liège. Photo © Samuel Nicolaï, on the occasion of the release of <italic>Alissia Lone</italic>
biography.cinemaDesc: Away from these stupid controversies, I later regret not having properly said goodbye to cinema. I start Casting, a painstaking work that aims to fill this gap and to somehow close my professional life as a critic. Beyond its cinephile thesis, this book reads like a tribute to women, to actresses and to my dragonfly. In 2006, the docu-fiction is still published by Somnambule Équivoque, a house that is decidedly dear to me, as we form a community of writers (Nicolas Crousse, Fidéline Dujeu, Christine Aventin, Nathalie Gassel…) where complicity reigns over competition, where the work of style and narration prevails over the search for a bestseller formula.
biography.bookDesc: Despite my black sheep postures, these first three books earned me being noticed by publishers located in the mainstream orbit. Luc Pire, great strategist of Belgian publishing, and Pierre François, director of Standard de Liège, offer me the incredible chance to sing the praises of my living gods in red and white jerseys. The documentary entitled La Tribu des Rouches will be one of the bookstore successes of the year 2007.
biography.caption2: During the writing of “La Tribu des Rouches”, I pose under the stadium lights, where my father took me to spend Saturday nights of my childhood.
biography.novelDesc: The omnipotent drivers of Camion Blanc, a French publishing house that carries rock, also seek to enlist my services. A year later, the biography <italic>Diva Siouxsie</italic> sets out in search of the multiple personalities of my very dear idol, yes, yes, the one I followed back then as a teenager groupie.
biography.inspiration: Meanwhile, my father passed away. It's a first shock. Travels (Malta, Italy, Greece, Croatia, France, Turkey, Canada) help me take a step back and, once my ideas are back in place, I re-enter my small office, I re-settle in my bubble, and here starts the most autobiographical of my novels. Funérarium, the title doesn't lie about the source of inspiration. In 2009, the book’s release at Somnambule Équivoque remains basically quite confidential. People read less and less. The publishing house seems to be in retreat, by dint of fighting against the currents. I still consider this novel as my most accomplished work, the one whose writing thickened my armor.
biography.writingCaption: A book signing at the Brussels Book Fair
biography.writingDesc1: I begin writing <italic>Patti Smith, the daughter of Rimbaud</italic>, a commission from Éditions Camion Blanc, imagining an enchanted interlude. It’s rather the opposite that occurs. My mother is diagnosed with Lewy body dementia, a nasty disease: hallucinatory and degenerative.
biography.writingDesc2: It's a second shock, so violent! In this context, inspiration is pushy: the painstaking investigative work, yet necessary for the biography, weighs on me. Fortunately, Muse Christina supports me and pushes me not to let go of this rigor that is mine. I still need four years to finish a portrait whose publication at the end of 2013 precedes by a few months my mother's death and its caravan of regrets (I stupidly never showed how much I loved her).
biography.exitDesc1: Just as leaving cultural journalism had seemed obvious to me in the early 2000s, fleeing literature now seems like the new obvious. It's necessary to change houses, change skins, finish the last sighs of innocence and let wings grow that transport to other continents, in search of endless journeys.
biography.exitDesc2: While Christina and I move from the Naniot district to the Péralta district, I become nomadic, and a photographer by extension. Every panorama, every building, every pretty young lady crossed at a turn of a path becomes a pretext for a shot. I walk untiringly, hunting for beautiful unknowns and sunsets in order to capture moments of eternity. This new passion forces me to walk long and slowly, to look without ever rushing, to scrutinize the smallest detail, to smile.
biography.caption3: In Canada, at Sutton, in 2015. The camera replaced the pen.
biography.exitDesc3: Basically, life is beautiful. What's horrible is having to leave it one day, or to see the people you love leave. Between these unspeakable pains, you have to have fun, there is no other possible choice!
biography.NBSTitle: The molting into Nomad Black Sheep
biography.NBSDesc1: From as far back as I can remember, I have always been a black sheep. At school. At university. By my way of learning, an autodidact now training in digital retouching software and video editing. In relation to the work that the self-righteous call “virtue” and that I consider as modern slavery with financial chains having replaced metal chains.
biography.NBSDesc2: Through my cinephile tastes, from Larry Clark to Harmony Korine. Through my literary tastes, from Elfriede Jelinek to Chloé Delaume. In my artistic choices that seek no one's assent. In my refusal to use social networks, preferring secret alcoves to exposed stages.
biography.NBSDesc3: Ana, a young Romanian woman who lives in Bucharest, enlightens me on my personality. She offers me a painting of a black sheep that will soon serve as a pseudonym for the pseudonym Étienne Éthaire: Nomad because, from Vietnam to Jamaica, from Seychelles to Philippines, I explore continents deeper and deeper. And Black Sheep, because “you think out of the box”, she assures.
biography.RLDesc1: Intelligence on the alert, sensitivity skin-deep, Ana possesses this rare talent of muse that she combines with her own talent as a tattoo artist. The tattooist makes me understand that I might not have said everything during my literary period, or at the very least, that I had left by fleeing, without presenting correct farewells, as with cinema before.
biography.RLDesc2: And since only muses have this power to push towards writing, Ana inspires me a novel so personal that I write it in the “Masculine I”. I no longer hide under a fictional character, I now undress myself, my soul terribly exposed like my skin offered to the tattoo machine. But if the nudity of bodies is nothing shocking, that of souls is much more unsettling…
biography.TattooCaption: Physical nudity should not be scary (‘Nude is not porn”). What is scary, however, is to lay bare the souls.
biography.endSection: I then divide myself between writing, travel photography and restoration of old photos. Incidentally, I inaugurate a Youtube channel thanks to the realization of a music video in tribute to… Siouxsie. I disperse. Sentimentally, artistically… Is this the cardinal energy that allows living several lives at once? Or is it the energy of despair when the bad wind of advancing age whips?
biography.desireDesc1: In my head, I am still twenty, and one is not serious when one is 20, the poets say. The body alas does not vibrate with the same carefreeness. With La Muse, since it is a farewell to literature, I have the distressing impression of completing a testamentary work. The last word. The text where the last questions will appear, necessarily without answer. I finish this novel with pride, but without knowing if I will agree to share it with the public. Later, maybe? Approaching the fateful moment? Or posthumously?
biography.desireDesc2: I opt for another solution: a confidential release, reserved for a restricted circle. Thus, I stay hidden, I avoid the excesses of the society of the spectacle. I don't need money. And I don't want any fame. I would hate to display my life on social networks. Éditions Lola Valérie Stein, directed by my friends Xavier and Marie-Anne (the daughter of Jean-Pierre Closson) accept all my conditions, respect all my criteria. In a few years, as late as possible, they will be able to manage my literary heritage.
biography.desireDesc3: Otherwise, I may still nourish some projects. Okay, I don't want to write any more novels, but nothing forbids me to prepare a book of photographs under which I can still write an anecdote here, a legend there. Writing travel chronicles rejuvenates me, nineteen in the head now. Besides, I almost no longer listen to rock, but today's sounds: hip-hop, electro, deep house.
biography.desireDesc4: These renewed tastes, chill music trend, are found on my Youtube channel which, without doing it on purpose, has gained momentum to the point that I no longer create the videos myself! Now, I delegate to more gifted editors than me, better technicians. A role of artistic manager suits me better. Thus Nomad Black Sheep becomes a production house that organizes shootings and even refines its branding by creating clothes.
biography.loveDesc1: Being a businessman, like being an artist, has anyway no other interest than the ephemeral search for pleasure. Otherwise, it's only vanity… On the other hand, what opposes vanity is an event of January 2023… Nora entered my misanthropic life. She is the little dog I always wanted to have, the one who forms the perfect duo with me, and who forces me to unprecedented responsibilities. And with Christina, whom I would no longer qualify as a muse but rather as an eternal accomplice, we form a pack. We are now united and reunited by what is pure, most respectful, most loyal: the love of a dog.
biography.caption4: Nora: beauty, intelligence and kindness gathered in a single being. Dogs should rule the world, not men!
biography.noraDesc1: A last word, in conclusion. For those who are looking for a less subjective and less emotional biography, they can eventually refer to a Wikipedia page or to the ultra-competence of an artificial intelligence.
biography.noraDesc2: I neither confirm nor infirm what may be written there, in fact, I don't know, I have never dared to read. I stick to this biography that I will complete as I go, for the longest, truly the longest possible. And when I have nothing more to hope for, there will be in fine an ultimate hope, that of dying by suicide. Scheduled date, who knows?, to be kept secret, like the day of birth.
biography.modalDescription: The next image in the progression is a <bold>full-body photograph</bold> intended to display the entirety of the tattoo work. As this image contains <bold>nudity</bold>, we require your explicit consent to view it.
biography.modalConsent: Click only if you are 18 or over.

cinema.title: My Cinema Library
cinema.quote: "Cinema is the modern writing whose ink is light." — Jean Cocteau
cinema.noMovies: No movies found for this search.

nbsPage.title: NOMAD BLACK SHEEP PRODUCTIONS
nbsPage.quote1: “The worst form of cowardice is conformity. Even a dead fish can follow the current.” <bold>Jim Hightower</bold>
nbsPage.quote2: “Invent yourself and then reinvent yourself and stay out of the clutches of mediocrity... Change your tone and shape so often that they can never categorize you.” <bold>Charles Bukowski</bold>
nbsPage.quote3: “I love art. And by art, I mean literature, music, painting… but also cooking, sex, the human body… Everything that is beautiful and good is art to me.” <bold>Jean Cocteau</bold>
nbsPage.caption1: This painting became the first logo of my YouTube channel
nbsPage.p1: As explained in the biography, the pseudonym Étienne Éthaire is now progressively giving way to the nickname Nomad Black Sheep, which emerged from a miniature painting by Ana Serafim and eventually established itself as a brand in its own right.
nbsPage.p2: Initially, I founded the Nomad Black Sheep YouTube channel with Colombe Akimana, a particularly gifted video editor. We were two editors... But, from the first music clips, our respective tastes and talents shaped our missions. Colombe naturally specialized in editing, where her technique excels. And, on my side, I naturally took control of artistic management, where my critical sense, high standards, and general culture serve as guidance.
nbsPage.p3: Anyway, I won't retrace the history of what quickly became a full-fledged production house here. My biography already mentions the genesis and evolution of a project that is in any case presented in detail on the Nomad Black Sheep website.
nbsPage.p4: On the other hand, what I want to express on this page is the inner will and the values that determine the Nomad Black Sheep concept. Starting with a clarification: I fight against short formats. I fight against superficiality. I advocate for depth and nuance. From there, I will not change my policy of deliberate absence from the communities offered by Facebook, Instagram, or TikTok. Certainly, I know the importance of social networks in modern marketing; nevertheless, I say 'shit' to them.
nbsPage.p5: There, it's written! One of the values of Nomad Black Sheep is to prove that it is possible to create artistic value without subscribing to the dictates of an extremely mercantile society. The rejection of Facebook, Instagram, and TikTok platforms falls within this framework. On the other hand, YouTube and websites are endorsed. YouTube is a medium! We do not use it as a social network and we reject that label. Similarly, the website is conceived as a catalog accessible to the entire world.
nbsPage.p6: With this preamble clarified, I would like to present my personal goal through Nomad Black Sheep. After years of intense creativity and travel around the world, my wish is now to become more of a producer than an artist. My pleasure is fueled by the desire to offer a platform of expression to young creators, whether they are writers (the Lola Valérie Stein Editions project), videographers, photographers, designers, or webmasters.
nbsPage.caption2: Authoritarianisms demonstrate the weakness of people with ideas as narrow as their brains. This is, in essence, what this image proclaims, corresponding to the mindset of the Nomad Black Sheeps.
nbsPage.p7: Ideally, I would even like to extend these production activities to cinema and music, but at this stage, one must remain realistic: Nomad Black Sheep does not have the necessary means to undertake quality production in these fields. The demand for quality is the absolute priority and does not currently allow for further dispersion.
nbsPage.p8: Next, a second goal goes beyond my personal ambition and joins that of a collective of artists now grouped under the Nomad Black Sheep label. It is a societal goal that represents values and illustrates struggles that are increasingly necessary in a society where borders, nationalisms, identity claims, and abuses of power do more than just scare us.
nbsPage.p9: Let it be clear: Nomad Black Sheep is not involved in politics. Neither right-wing nor left-wing, a black sheep hates fascisms, communisms, fundamentalisms, and all authoritarianisms—intentionally cited here in the plural—equally. He never marches in step; he subscribes to no ideology. He traverses the world, as he pleases and at his own pace, fighting for all freedoms of expression, for gender equality, and for an art freed from the desires for censorship that lurk around its green meadows...
nbsPage.caption3: We live in an increasingly standardized society, where all rough edges and anything that stands out must be smoothed over. We face self-righteousness and political correctness. We face a lack of kindness, a lack of solidarity, a lack of generosity. Against these terrifying observations, we oppose this 'Fuck normality' as an amusing and iconoclastic slogan.
nbsPage.p10: There are dozens of ways to be a black sheep. If the mindset displayed on this page touches you, if 'freedom', 'tolerance', and 'generosity' are among your favorite words in the dictionary, then we can work together and build bridges. On our side, our promise will be to maintain a professional quality standard and practice accessible prices. It's your move to start a collaboration.

photographyEroticism.title: Eroticism
photographyEroticism.quote1: “Eroticism lies in the possibility of a gesture. It belongs to the realm of dreams.” <bold>Jean-François Somain</bold>
photographyEroticism.quote2: “Eroticism, this triumph of dream over nature, is the high refuge of the spirit of poetry.” <bold>Emmanuelle Arsan</bold>
photographyEroticism.intro: As much as I hate pornography—a disgusting reflection of our society, crude and vulgar—I venerate eroticism, its delicacy, its invitation to the celebration of life. Since one can never tire of admiring beauty, I present to you some masterpieces from my private collection.

photographyTravel.title: Travel
photographyTravel.quote: "Like everything that matters in life, a beautiful journey is a work of art" <bold>André Suarès in The Condottiere's Journey.</bold>
photographyTravel.intro: I would like to pin the countries visited. In the meantime, I propose to make a photographic census, country by country, in alphabetical order.

photographyIntro.title: Introduction
photographyIntro.quote: 'Photography is a picture painted by the sun without artistic instruction', <bold>Ambrose Bierce</bold>
photographyIntro.p1: I might as well say it upfront: I am not a professional photographer, far from it. In my youth, I even traveled without a camera; I would occasionally buy a disposable Kodak at a souvenir kiosk, and that cardboard junk was enough for me, as only Cinema and Literature mattered, with a capital C and a capital L. I didn't grant the status of the capital letter to photography; I didn't even understand that a still image, beyond its possible aesthetics, also tells a story. What an idiot! Today, I stand corrected: Photography deserves a huge P. It freezes time, which is pure magic for a dreamer who would want to live forever.
photographyIntro.p2: With experience and the accessibility of high-performance equipment, I have progressively improved in framing and technique.
photographyIntro.p3: And when I discovered digital retouching software, it was a game-changer—divine intervention that suddenly made all my old failed photos look 'at least almost beautiful.' Today, the miracle continues thanks to artificial intelligence.
photographyIntro.p4: Thus, photography has become my globetrotter's weapon. I no longer go anywhere without my state-of-the-art camera, integrated into my phone and slipped into my pocket for more ease.
photographyIntro.p5: New mobile phones have become so powerful that it's possible to travel light, dragging a wheeled suitcase—in short, 'on the road again,' but according to the beatnik slogan resurrected in the bourgeois fashion of the 21st century. The digital camera and Photoshop have thus replaced the spiral notebook in the pocket. Behind it, the philosophy remains the same: that of a nomadic black sheep seeking encounters with other people, other cultures, other landscapes...
photographyIntro.p6: And photography has become the umbilical cord between my roots and my traces of exile. For there are a multitude of 'elsewheres,' but there is also Liège, home sweet home, my beloved birthplace.

photographyPage.title: Photography and Travel
photographyPage.intro1: I might as well say it upfront: I am not a professional photographer, far from it. In my youth, I even traveled without a camera; I would occasionally buy a disposable Kodak at a souvenir kiosk, and that cardboard junk was enough for me, as only Cinema and Literature mattered, with a capital C and a capital L. I didn't grant the status of the capital letter to photography; I didn't even understand that a still image, beyond its possible aesthetics, also tells a story. What an idiot! Today, I stand corrected: Photography deserves a huge P. It freezes time, which is pure magic for a dreamer who would want to live forever.

photos.caption: Memory of my new wave years, concerts in London, my fan road trip to follow my idol, Siouxsie, with her Banshees
In London, everything breathes music, from Carnaby Street to the subway corridors
Poppies as far as the eye can see, so many childhood memories, I love this flower, red like my Standard

These first three photos represent the Burial of Mati L'Ohé, a joyous, pagan, iconoclastic, tumultuous, disheveled celebration, fueled by péket (local gin). Every August 16th, the procession and ball conclude three days and nights of festivities. I advise everyone to come and discover the spirit of Liège.
At the time, I was a poor photographer equipped with the worst cameras, the disposable Kodaks. Nonetheless, this is a historic photo of a vanished New York, given the presence of the Twin Towers
Twenty years later in Chicago, I finally became a good photographer, with a sharp eye and the camera always slung over my shoulder
New Year celebrations according to the Lao calendar. Everyone throws water on everyone.
The joys of swimming in the river doubled by the immense beauty of Carolina's breasts
All animals are my friends. And, as the t-shirt says: 'you're your only limit'
One of the many joys in Asian cities: drinking a cocktail at rooftop lounge bars.
In those late 80s, it was still the old communist Czechoslovakia under Moscow's thumb. After visiting it, it was no longer possible to believe in the pipe dreams of communism.
Czechoslovakia split into two. In the capital, Prague, a new wind is blowing, distorting and reviving the facades.

It is a country that, officially, does not exist. A small strip of Russia in Moldova, a self-proclaimed independent territory strictly guarded by Russian soldiers. I hate the Kremlin regime and its vassals, but hey, if one believes in the free self-determination of peoples, the philosophy applies both ways...
